Segue copia: # BMad Method & BMad Core [![Stable Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/bmad-method?color=blue&label=stable)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-method) [![Alpha Version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/bmad-method/alpha?color=orange&label=alpha)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-method) [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](LICENSE) [![Node.js Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/node-%3E%3D20.0.0-brightgreen)](https://nodejs.org) [![Discord](https://img.shields.io/badge/Discord-Join%20Community-7289da?logo=discord&logoColor=white)](https://discord.gg/gk8jAdXWmj) ---
## 🎉 NEW: BMAD V6 Installer - Create & Share Custom Content! The completely revamped **BMAD V6 installer** now includes built-in support for creating, installing, and sharing custom modules, agents, workflows, templates, and tools! Build your own AI solutions or share them with your team - and real soon, with the whole BMad Community througha verified community sharing portal! **✨ What's New:** - 📦 **Streamlined Custom Module Installation** - Package your custom content as installable modules - 🤖 **Agent & Workflow Sharing** - Distribute standalone agents and workflows - 🔄 **Unitary Module Support** - Install individual components without full modules - ⚙️ **Dependency Management** - Automatic handling of module dependencies - 🛡️ **Update-Safe Customization** - Your custom content persists through updates **📚 Learn More:** - [**Custom Content Overview**](./docs/custom-content.md) - Discover all supported content types - [**Installation Guide**](./docs/custom-content-installation.md) - Learn to create and install custom content - [**Detail Content Docs**](./src/modules/bmb/docs/index.md) - Reference details for agents, modules, workflows and the bmad builder - [**2 Very simple Custom Modules of questionable quality**](./samples/sample-custom-modules/README.md) - if you want to download and try to install a custom shared module, get an idea of how to bundle and share your own, or create your own personal agents, workflows and modules.
--- ## AI-Driven Agile Development That Scales From Bug Fixes to Enterprise **Build More, Architect Dreams** (BMAD) with **21 specialized AI agents** across 4 official modules, and **50+ guided workflows** that adapt to your project's complexity—from quick bug fixes to enterprise platforms, and new step file workflows that allow for incredibly long workflows to stay on the rails longer than ever before! Additionally - when we say 'Build More, Architect Dreams' - we mean it! The BMad Builder has landed, and now as of Alpha.15 is fully supported in the installation flow via NPX - custom stand along agents, workflows and the modules of your dreams! The community forge will soon open, endless possibility awaits! > **🚀 v6 is a MASSIVE upgrade from v4!** Complete architectural overhaul, scale-adaptive intelligence, visual workflows, and the powerful BMad Core framework. v4 users: this changes everything. [See what's new →](#whats-new-in-v6) > **📌 v6 Alpha Status:** Near-beta quality with vastly improved stability. Documentation is being finalized. New videos coming soon to [BMadCode YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@BMadCode). ## 🎯 Why BMad Method? Unlike generic AI coding assistants, BMad Method provides **structured, battle-tested workflows** powered by specialized agents who understand agile development. Each agent has deep domain expertise—from product management to architecture to testing—working together seamlessly. **✨ Key Benefits:** - **Scale-Adaptive Intelligence** - Automatically adjusts planning depth from bug fixes to enterprise systems - **Complete Development Lifecycle** - Analysis → Planning → Architecture → Implementation - **Specialized Expertise** - 19 agents with specific roles (PM, Architect, Developer, UX Designer, etc.) - **Proven Methodologies** - Built on agile best practices with AI amplification - **IDE Integration** - Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code ## 🏗️ The Power of BMad Core **BMad Method** is actually a sophisticated module built on top of **BMad Core** (**C**ollaboration **O**ptimized **R**eflection **E**ngine). This revolutionary architecture means: - **BMad Core** provides the universal framework for human-AI collaboration - **BMad Method** leverages Core to deliver agile development workflows - **BMad Builder** lets YOU create custom modules as powerful as BMad Method itself With **BMad Builder**, you can architect both simple agents and vastly complex domain-specific modules (legal, medical, finance, education, creative) that will soon be sharable in an **official community marketplace**. Imagine building and sharing your own specialized AI team! ## 📊 See It In Action

Complete BMad Method workflow showing all phases, agents, and decision points

## 🚀 Get Started in 3 Steps ### 1. Install BMad Method ```bash # Install v6 Alpha (recommended) npx bmad-method@alpha install # Or stable v4 for production npx bmad-method install ``` ### 2. Initialize Your Project Load any agent in your IDE and run: ``` *workflow-init ``` This analyzes your project and recommends the right workflow track. ### 3. Choose Your Track BMad Method adapts to your needs with three intelligent tracks: | Track | Use For | Planning | Time to Start | | ------------------ | ------------------------- | ----------------------- | ------------- | | **⚡ Quick Flow** | Bug fixes, small features | Tech spec only | < 5 minutes | | **📋 BMad Method** | Products, platforms | PRD + Architecture + UX | < 15 minutes | | **🏢 Enterprise** | Compliance, scale | Full governance suite | < 30 minutes | > **Not sure?** Run `*workflow-init` and let BMad analyze your project goal. ## 🔄 How It Works: 4-Phase Methodology BMad Method guides you through a proven development lifecycle: 1. **📊 Analysis** (Optional) - Brainstorm, research, and explore solutions 2. **📝 Planning** - Create PRDs, tech specs, or game design documents 3. **🏗️ Solutioning** - Design architecture, UX, and technical approach 4. **⚡ Implementation** - Story-driven development with continuous validation Each phase has specialized workflows and agents working together to deliver exceptional results. ## 🤖 Meet Your Team **12 Specialized Agents** working in concert: | Development | Architecture | Product | Leadership | | ----------- | -------------- | ------------- | -------------- | | Developer | Architect | PM | Scrum Master | | UX Designer | Test Architect | Analyst | BMad Master | | Tech Writer | Game Architect | Game Designer | Game Developer | **Test Architect** integrates with `@seontechnologies/playwright-utils` for production-ready fixture-based utilities. Each agent brings deep expertise and can be customized to match your team's style. ## 📦 What's Included ### Core Modules - **BMad Method (BMM)** - Complete agile development framework - 12 specialized agents - 34 workflows across 4 phases - Scale-adaptive planning - [→ Documentation Hub](./src/modules/bmm/docs/index.md) - **BMad Builder (BMB)** - Create custom agents and workflows - Build anything from simple agents to complex modules - Create domain-specific solutions (legal, medical, finance, education) - [→ Builder Guide](./src/modules/bmb/docs/index.md) - **Creative Intelligence Suite (CIS)** - Innovation & problem-solving - Brainstorming, design thinking, storytelling - 5 creative facilitation workflows - [→ Creative Workflows](./src/modules/cis/docs/index.md) ### Key Features - **🎨 Customizable Agents** - Modify personalities, expertise, and communication styles - **🌐 Multi-Language Support** - Separate settings for communication and code output - **📄 Document Sharding** - 90% token savings for large projects - **🔄 Update-Safe** - Your customizations persist through updates - **🚀 Web Bundles** - Use in ChatGPT, Claude Projects, or Gemini Gems ## 📚 Documentation ### Quick Links - **[Quick Start Guide](./src/modules/bmm/docs/quick-start.md)** - 15-minute introduction - **[Complete BMM Documentation](./src/modules/bmm/docs/index.md)** - All guides and references - **[Agent Customization](./docs/agent-customization-guide.md)** - Personalize your agents - **[All Documentation](./docs/index.md)** - Complete documentation index ### For v4 Users - **[v4 Documentation](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD/tree/V4)** - **[v4 to v6 Upgrade Guide](./docs/v4-to-v6-upgrade.md)** ## 💬 Community & Support - **[Discord Community](https://discord.gg/gk8jAdXWmj)** - Get help, share projects - **[GitHub Issues](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD/issues)** - Report bugs, request features - **[YouTube Channel](https://www.youtube.com/@BMadCode)** - Video tutorials and demos - **[Web Bundles](https://bmad-code-org.github.io/bmad-bundles/)** - Pre-built agent bundles - **[Code of Conduct](.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)** - Community guidelines ## 🛠️ Development For contributors working on the BMad codebase: ```bash # Run all quality checks npm test # Development commands npm run lint:fix # Fix code style npm run format:fix # Auto-format code npm run bundle # Build web bundles ``` See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for full development guidelines. ## What's New in v6 **v6 represents a complete architectural revolution from v4:** ### 🚀 Major Upgrades - **BMad Core Framework** - Modular architecture enabling custom domain solutions - **Scale-Adaptive Intelligence** - Automatic adjustment from bug fixes to enterprise - **Visual Workflows** - Beautiful SVG diagrams showing complete methodology - **BMad Builder Module** - Create and share your own AI agent teams - **50+ Workflows** - Up from 20 in v4, covering every development scenario - **19 Specialized Agents** - Enhanced with customizable personalities and expertise - **Update-Safe Customization** - Your configs persist through all updates - **Web Bundles** - Use agents in ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini - **Multi-Language Support** - Separate settings for communication and code - **Document Sharding** - 90% token savings for large projects ### 🔄 For v4 Users - **[Comprehensive Upgrade Guide](./docs/v4-to-v6-upgrade.md)** - Step-by-step migration - **[v4 Documentation Archive](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD/tree/V4)** - Legacy reference - Backwards compatibility where possible - Smooth migration path with installer detection ## 📄 License MIT License - See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details. **Trademarks:** BMad™ and BMAD-METHOD™ are trademarks of BMad Code, LLC. # BMAD Method V6 Documentation Index Summary

## 1. Available Documentation Files

**Project-Level Documentation:**
- Main repository overview and feature summary with module introductions
- Contribution guidelines including pull request standards and code style requirements
- Version history documenting breaking changes across releases

**Setup & Migration:**
- Upgrade pathway for v4 users transitioning to v6
- Token optimization guide for splitting large documents
- Maintainer guide for automated bundle publishing (currently unavailable)

## 2. Installation & Setup Process

The documentation recommends a **situation-based onboarding approach**:

- **New users:** Begin with the Quick Start Guide, then progress to workflow documentation
- **v4 migrants:** Review the upgrade guide first, then complete the Quick Start
- **Legacy projects:** Use the brownfield-specific guide before standard quickstart

**Key setup resource:** "Step-by-step guide to building your first project" covers initial configuration.

## 3. Module Architecture

**BMad Method (BMM)** - Core development module
- Supports agile AI-driven software and game development
- Includes 5-level scaling system
- Specialized testing and quality assurance framework

**BMad Builder (BMB)** - Customization platform
- Enables creation of custom agents and workflows
- Allows module extension and personalization

**Creative Intelligence Suite (CIS)** - Innovation tools
- Provides AI-powered brainstorming capabilities

## 4. Key Configuration & Customization

- Custom agent installer enables personalization
- Agent behavior can be modified through dedicated customization guides
- Workflow structures are documented for each module

## 5. Upgrade & Migration Paths

Six documented progression paths exist, including specific routes for:
- Software projects
- Game development
- v4-to-v6 transitions
- Brownfield (existing codebase) scenarios
- Custom solution builders
- Project contributors

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me provide you with a complete exploration report:

---

## EXPLORATION REPORT: MMS Project Documentation

I've successfully explored the `/docs/` folder and analyzed the complete project context. Here's what I found:

### **1. Documentation Files Present**

The `docs/` directory contains 6 key files:

- `/docs/README.md` — Domain overview and system architecture (252 lines)
- `/docs/MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md` — Comprehensive 1,500+ line playbook covering all project aspects
- `/docs/MMS_PROFESSIONAL_PROFILES.md` — Team hiring requirements (700+ lines)
- `/docs/BMAD_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` — BMAD methodology integration (950 lines)
- `/docs/OLD_LEGACY_RESEARCH.md` — Historical research (legacy reference)
- `/docs/RFQ.pdf` — Official request for proposal document

### **2. Project Context and Objectives**

**Project Name:** MMS - Metro Mileage Management System

**Mission:** Enterprise solution to centralize and automate mileage management for mixed rolling stock fleets (>100 units) in a railway network.

**Core Objective (from Playbook §1.2-1.3):**
> Create functional demo in 3 months that demonstrates technical competence and proves understanding of the railway domain. Success metric: "After 15 minutes of demo, the client thinks: They understand our problem better than the others."

**What MMS Actually Does:**
- Replaces manual workflows with rule-based, predictive engine
- Optimizes preventative maintenance scheduling
- Balances mileage across fleet
- Provides unified interface between Maintenance and Operations teams

### **3. Key System Outputs**

From README.md §System Outputs:

| Output Type | Format | Purpose |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| Dashboard | Web UI | Fleet-wide mileage status, KPIs |
| Maintenance Status | Web UI | Current position per train vs. next PM/overhaul |
| Run Assignments | Web UI, Export | Daily train allocation recommendations |
| Maintenance Schedule | Web UI, Export | Completed and upcoming PM/overhaul activities |
| Compliance Status | Web UI | Real-time adherence to maintenance requirements |
| Cost Compliance | Web UI, Export | Actual vs. planned maintenance costs |
| Budget Forecast | Web UI, Export | Projected maintenance costs per fleet |
| Simulation Reports | PDF, Excel | Scenario comparison for cost/risk/wear distribution |
| Notifications | Push, Email | Alerts via in-app and email |

### **4. BMAD Methodology Integration**

**File:** `/docs/BMAD_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` (37KB, comprehensive)

The project uses **BMAD V6 (Build More, Architect Dreams)** methodology with:

- **4-Phase Workflow:** Analysis → Planning → Solutioning → Implementation
- **21 Specialized AI Agents** for different roles
- **Scale-Adaptive Planning** (Level 2-3 for MMS)
- **12-Week Timeline** structured as 6 sprints

**BMAD Structure:**
```
_bmad/
├── bmm/ (BMad Method Module)
│   ├── agents/ (8 standard agents)
│   └── workflows/ (4 phases)
├── custom/agents/ (2 custom agents for railway domain)
├── project-context.md
└── bmm-workflow-status.yaml

bmad-artifacts/
├── phase-1-analysis/
├── phase-2-planning/
├── phase-3-solutioning/ (with epics/ and stories/)
└── phase-4-implementation/ (with sprint folders)
```

### **5. Project Context and Objectives from Playbook**

**Why This Matters (§1.1-1.4):**
- Client is a European railway operator evaluating multiple vendors
- The company (software developer) is NOT a railway specialist
- Winning the bid requires proving understanding + technical competence
- Focus: **1 pain point solved well > 10 pain points poorly**

**Not Included (Intentional Scope Reduction):**
- Production system (demo only)
- Complete MVP (focused demo)
- Real EAM integration (mocked data sufficient)
- Solution for all problems

### **6. Railway Domain Knowledge**

**From Playbook §2 (Mandatory Reading):**

**Critical Terms:**
- **Rolling Stock** — Trains, locomotives, wagons (main managed entities)
- **Consist** — Composition of units forming a train
- **Dead Running** — Movement without passengers/cargo (cost to minimize)
- **PM1/PM2/HM/Overhaul** — Maintenance levels with intervals (10K-6M km, 6-12 years)
- **Wheelset** — Most critical component for balancing (flange < 28.5mm = mandatory reprofiling)
- **Bogie** — Highest MTTR, single point of failure component
- **Homologation** — Train certification for route/country (hard constraint)

**Unknown Unknowns (Critical Discoveries §2.4):**
1. Wheelset wear is **non-linear** (balancing only total km is insufficient)
2. Bogie MTTR: 4-72 hours (highest availability impact)
3. Consist/married pairs are inseparable
4. Crew constraints: route knowledge, traction certification, Hours of Service
5. Shunting: 15-30 min per maneuver, ~€350 cost
6. Operational times ignored in naive plans: sign-on (15-30m), walk (5-15m), prep (10-20m), secure (5-15m)

### **7. Demo Strategy**

**From Playbook §4:**

**Main Differentiators:**
1. Km balancing as **primary** objective (competitors treat as secondary)
2. Unified Maintenance ↔ Operations interface (eliminates silos)
3. Visible dead running cost (hidden cost made explicit)
4. Accessible what-if simulation (decision-makers can "play")
5. Visible decision justification (trust, not black box)

**Market Position:**
- **Tier 1 Competitors:** DELMIA Quintiq, zedas®asset, Railfleet, TRACS-RS
- **Weakness to exploit:** Dated UX, weak Maintenance-Operations integration, no open-source alternative

### **8. Functional Requirements**

**From Playbook §5 (8 epics planned):**

| Epic | Stories | Priority | Focus |
|------|---------|----------|-------|
| E1: Dashboard & KPIs | 8-10 | Must Have | Fleet overview, status indicators |
| E2: Interactive Map | 6-8 | Must Have | Train positions, clustering, zoom |
| E3: Mileage Balancing | 8-10 | Must Have | Balance visualization, targets |
| E4: Train Allocation | 10-12 | Must Have | Run assignments, solver integration |
| E5: What-If Simulation | 8-10 | Must Have | Scenario comparison, impact analysis |
| E6: Gantt Maintenance | 6-8 | Should Have | Timeline, drag-drop scheduling |
| E7: Alerts System | 4-6 | Should Have | Real-time notifications |
| E8: Seed Data & Demo | 4-6 | Must Have | Realistic test data, demo scripts |

**Total: 50-60 stories across 8 epics**

### **9. Technical Architecture**

**From Playbook §8:**

**Core Components:**
1. **Frontend** — React/Vue web app (dashboard, map, forms)
2. **Backend API** — REST/GraphQL (data exposure, run assignments)
3. **OR-Tools Solver** — Python optimization engine (allocation algorithm)
4. **Cache Layer** — Redis (quick lookups, simulation cache)
5. **Database** — PostgreSQL (train, fleet, maintenance, cost data)
6. **EAM Integration** — API-based connector (IBM Maximo, SAP PM)

**Solver Logic:**
- Constraint-based optimization (OR-Tools/CPLEX)
- Constraints: Train fitness, maintenance windows, resource limits, mileage balance
- Objective: Minimize mileage variance while respecting hard constraints

### **10. Timeline and Team Structure**

**From Playbook §10 and Professional Profiles:**

**12-Week Delivery (3 months):**
- **Weeks 1-2:** Setup, Analysis, Planning
- **Weeks 3-4:** Architecture, UX Design, Epic/Story Creation
- **Weeks 5-6:** Sprint 1 (Core Solver + API)
- **Weeks 7-8:** Sprint 2 (Frontend Base)
- **Weeks 9-10:** Sprint 3 (Integration + Domain Validation)
- **Weeks 11-12:** Sprint 4 (Demo Polish + Rehearsal)

**Team Composition (from Professional Profiles §6):**
- **Railway Domain Expert** (Rolling Stock Engineer) — 20 hours
- **Railway Operations Planner** — 40 hours
- **Backend Developer** — Full-time
- **Frontend Developer** — Full-time
- **UX Designer** — Full-time
- **Tech Lead** — Full-time
- **Demo Coordinator** — 40 hours (weeks 10-12)

### **11. Risks and Mitigations**

**From Playbook §11:**

| Risk | Impact | BMAD Mitigation |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| R1: Generic presentation | Low credibility | Railway Domain Expert validates terminology |
| R2: Solver too slow | Demo crashes | TEA agent continuous performance testing |
| R3: Unanswered questions | Client doubt | Demo Coordinator comprehensive FAQ |
| R4: Bugs during demo | Catastrophic | Continuous code review, rehearsals |
| R5: Scope creep | Missed deadline | PM guards backlog, SM manages sprint scope |
| R6: Team lacks domain knowledge | Poor solution | Phase 1 analysis consolidates knowledge |

### **12. Custom BMAD Agents**

**From BMAD Implementation Plan §5:**

**Standard Agents (8):**
- PM (John) — Product Manager
- Analyst — Research & Analysis
- Architect — System Design
- UX Designer — User Experience
- SM (Marcus) — Scrum Master
- DEV — Developer
- TEA — Test Architect
- Tech Writer — Documentation

**Custom Agents for Railway (2):**
- **Maria** (Railway Domain Expert) — Validates terminology, seed data, constraints
- **Carlos** (Demo Coordinator) — Presentation strategy, FAQ, script refinement

---

## Summary

The MMS project is a **well-documented, strategically-planned railway fleet management demo** aimed at winning a bid in 3 months. It combines:

1. **Deep Domain Knowledge** — Comprehensive glossary, operational constraints, "unknown unknowns"
2. **Market-Aware Strategy** — Differentiators against Tier 1 competitors
3. **Structured Methodology** — BMAD V6 with 4 phases, 8 epics, 50-60 stories
4. **Clear Success Metric** — Client perception: "They understand our problem better"
5. **Team Framework** — Roles, hours, responsibilities clearly defined

The playbook serves as the single source of truth for the entire project, from domain glossary through technical architecture to demo script (§12).

- Provides unified interface between Maintenance and Operations teams

### **3. Key System Outputs**

From README.md §System Outputs:

| Output Type | Format | Purpose |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| Dashboard | Web UI | Fleet-wide mileage status, KPIs |
| Maintenance Status | Web UI | Current position per train vs. next PM/overhaul |
| Run Assignments | Web UI, Export | Daily train allocation recommendations |
| Maintenance Schedule | Web UI, Export | Completed and upcoming PM/overhaul activities |
| Compliance Status | Web UI | Real-time adherence to maintenance requirements |
| Cost Compliance | Web UI, Export | Actual vs. planned maintenance costs |
| Budget Forecast | Web UI, Export | Projected maintenance costs per fleet |
| Simulation Reports | PDF, Excel | Scenario comparison for cost/risk/wear distribution |
| Notifications | Push, Email | Alerts via in-app and email |

### **4. BMAD Methodology Integration**

**File:** `/docs/BMAD_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` (37KB, comprehensive)

The project uses **BMAD V6 (Build More, Architect Dreams)** methodology with:

- **4-Phase Workflow:** Analysis → Planning → Solutioning → Implementation
- **21 Specialized AI Agents** for different roles
- **Scale-Adaptive Planning** (Level 2-3 for MMS)
- **12-Week Timeline** structured as 6 sprints

**BMAD Structure:**
```
_bmad/
├── bmm/ (BMad Method Module)
│   ├── agents/ (8 standard agents)
│   └── workflows/ (4 phases)
├── custom/agents/ (2 custom agents for railway domain)
├── project-context.md
└── bmm-workflow-status.yaml

bmad-artifacts/
├── phase-1-analysis/
├── phase-2-planning/
├── phase-3-solutioning/ (with epics/ and stories/)
└── phase-4-implementation/ (with sprint folders)
```

### **5. Project Context and Objectives from Playbook**

**Why This Matters (§1.1-1.4):**
- Client is a European railway operator evaluating multiple vendors
- The company (software developer) is NOT a railway specialist
- Winning the bid requires proving understanding + technical competence
- Focus: **1 pain point solved well > 10 pain points poorly**

**Not Included (Intentional Scope Reduction):**
- Production system (demo only)
- Complete MVP (focused demo)
- Real EAM integration (mocked data sufficient)
- Solution for all problems

### **6. Railway Domain Knowledge**

**From Playbook §2 (Mandatory Reading):**

**Critical Terms:**
- **Rolling Stock** — Trains, locomotives, wagons (main managed entities)
- **Consist** — Composition of units forming a train
- **Dead Running** — Movement without passengers/cargo (cost to minimize)
- **PM1/PM2/HM/Overhaul** — Maintenance levels with intervals (10K-6M km, 6-12 years)
- **Wheelset** — Most critical component for balancing (flange < 28.5mm = mandatory reprofiling)
- **Bogie** — Highest MTTR, single point of failure component
- **Homologation** — Train certification for route/country (hard constraint)

**Unknown Unknowns (Critical Discoveries §2.4):**
1. Wheelset wear is **non-linear** (balancing only total km is insufficient)
2. Bogie MTTR: 4-72 hours (highest availability impact)
3. Consist/married pairs are inseparable
4. Crew constraints: route knowledge, traction certification, Hours of Service
5. Shunting: 15-30 min per maneuver, ~€350 cost
6. Operational times ignored in naive plans: sign-on (15-30m), walk (5-15m), prep (10-20m), secure (5-15m)

### **7. Demo Strategy**

**From Playbook §4:**

**Main Differentiators:**
1. Km balancing as **primary** objective (competitors treat as secondary)
2. Unified Maintenance ↔ Operations interface (eliminates silos)
3. Visible dead running cost (hidden cost made explicit)
4. Accessible what-if simulation (decision-makers can "play")
5. Visible decision justification (trust, not black box)

**Market Position:**
- **Tier 1 Competitors:** DELMIA Quintiq, zedas®asset, Railfleet, TRACS-RS
- **Weakness to exploit:** Dated UX, weak Maintenance-Operations integration, no open-source alternative

### **8. Functional Requirements**

**From Playbook §5 (8 epics planned):**

| Epic | Stories | Priority | Focus |
|------|---------|----------|-------|
| E1: Dashboard & KPIs | 8-10 | Must Have | Fleet overview, status indicators |
| E2: Interactive Map | 6-8 | Must Have | Train positions, clustering, zoom |
| E3: Mileage Balancing | 8-10 | Must Have | Balance visualization, targets |
| E4: Train Allocation | 10-12 | Must Have | Run assignments, solver integration |
| E5: What-If Simulation | 8-10 | Must Have | Scenario comparison, impact analysis |
| E6: Gantt Maintenance | 6-8 | Should Have | Timeline, drag-drop scheduling |
| E7: Alerts System | 4-6 | Should Have | Real-time notifications |
| E8: Seed Data & Demo | 4-6 | Must Have | Realistic test data, demo scripts |

**Total: 50-60 stories across 8 epics**

### **9. Technical Architecture**

**From Playbook §8:**

**Core Components:**
1. **Frontend** — React/Vue web app (dashboard, map, forms)
2. **Backend API** — REST/GraphQL (data exposure, run assignments)
3. **OR-Tools Solver** — Python optimization engine (allocation algorithm)
4. **Cache Layer** — Redis (quick lookups, simulation cache)
5. **Database** — PostgreSQL (train, fleet, maintenance, cost data)
6. **EAM Integration** — API-based connector (IBM Maximo, SAP PM)

**Solver Logic:**
- Constraint-based optimization (OR-Tools/CPLEX)
- Constraints: Train fitness, maintenance windows, resource limits, mileage balance
- Objective: Minimize mileage variance while respecting hard constraints

### **10. Timeline and Team Structure**

**From Playbook §10 and Professional Profiles:**

**12-Week Delivery (3 months):**
- **Weeks 1-2:** Setup, Analysis, Planning
- **Weeks 3-4:** Architecture, UX Design, Epic/Story Creation
- **Weeks 5-6:** Sprint 1 (Core Solver + API)
- **Weeks 7-8:** Sprint 2 (Frontend Base)
- **Weeks 9-10:** Sprint 3 (Integration + Domain Validation)
- **Weeks 11-12:** Sprint 4 (Demo Polish + Rehearsal)

**Team Composition (from Professional Profiles §6):**
- **Railway Domain Expert** (Rolling Stock Engineer) — 20 hours
- **Railway Operations Planner** — 40 hours
- **Backend Developer** — Full-time
- **Frontend Developer** — Full-time
- **UX Designer** — Full-time
- **Tech Lead** — Full-time
- **Demo Coordinator** — 40 hours (weeks 10-12)

### **11. Risks and Mitigations**

**From Playbook §11:**

| Risk | Impact | BMAD Mitigation |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| R1: Generic presentation | Low credibility | Railway Domain Expert validates terminology |
| R2: Solver too slow | Demo crashes | TEA agent continuous performance testing |
| R3: Unanswered questions | Client doubt | Demo Coordinator comprehensive FAQ |
| R4: Bugs during demo | Catastrophic | Continuous code review, rehearsals |
| R5: Scope creep | Missed deadline | PM guards backlog, SM manages sprint scope |
| R6: Team lacks domain knowledge | Poor solution | Phase 1 analysis consolidates knowledge |

### **12. Custom BMAD Agents**

**From BMAD Implementation Plan §5:**

**Standard Agents (8):**
- PM (John) — Product Manager
- Analyst — Research & Analysis
- Architect — System Design
- UX Designer — User Experience
- SM (Marcus) — Scrum Master
- DEV — Developer
- TEA — Test Architect
- Tech Writer — Documentation

**Custom Agents for Railway (2):**
- **Maria** (Railway Domain Expert) — Validates terminology, seed data, constraints
- **Carlos** (Demo Coordinator) — Presentation strategy, FAQ, script refinement

---

## Summary

The MMS project is a **well-documented, strategically-planned railway fleet management demo** aimed at winning a bid in 3 months. It combines:

1. **Deep Domain Knowledge** — Comprehensive glossary, operational constraints, "unknown unknowns"
2. **Market-Aware Strategy** — Differentiators against Tier 1 competitors
3. **Structured Methodology** — BMAD V6 with 4 phases, 8 epics, 50-60 stories
4. **Clear Success Metric** — Client perception: "They understand our problem better"
5. **Team Framework** — Roles, hours, responsibilities clearly defined

The playbook serves as the single source of truth for the entire project, from domain glossary through technical architecture to demo script (§12).

# BMad v4 to v6 Upgrade Summary

## Major Changes

**Architecture Rewrite**: v6 represents a "complete ground-up rewrite with significant architectural changes." The most fundamental shift: v4's `_bmad-core` folder wasn't actually the core framework—it was the BMad Method itself. v6 separates these concerns into distinct modules.

## Folder Structure Transformation

**v4 Layout** (Expansion Pack Model):
- Separate folders for each capability (`_bmad-core`, `_bmad-game-dev`, `_bmad-creative-writing`)
- Mixed responsibilities within core folder

**v6 Layout** (Unified Installation):
```
_bmad/
├── _config/agents/        # All customizations here
├── core/                  # Universal framework
├── bmm/                   # BMad Method (software/game dev)
├── bmb/                   # BMad Builder (agent creation)
└── cis/                   # Creative Intelligence Suite
```

## Configuration Files

- **New requirement**: Each module includes a `config.yaml` file at its root
- **Customization hub**: Agent customizations moved to `_bmad/_config/agents/` using `.customize.yaml` files
- **Artifact organization**: Planning outputs go to `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/` (PRD, brief, architecture, UX)

## Deprecated Items

| v4 Module | v6 Status |
|-----------|-----------|
| `_bmad-*-game-dev` (all variants) | Consolidated into new BMGD Module |
| `_bmad-infrastructure-devops` | Deprecated; replacement in development |
| `_bmad-creative-writing` | Not adapted; new module coming |

## Migration Workflow

1. **Automatic backup**: Running `npm run install:bmad` detects v4 folders and moves them to `v4-backup/` with timestamps
2. **Manual cleanup**: Remove legacy IDE commands from `.claude/commands/BMad/` directories
3. **Document migration**: Move planning artifacts to standardized `_bmad-output/` structure
4. **Agent customization rebuild**: After modifying `.customize.yaml` files, re-run the installer and select rebuild option

**Recommendation**: If only partially through planning phases, restart with v6's optimized workflows for superior results.

content.content:
# BMM Documentation Index

## 1. Available Agents and Roles

The documentation references **12 specialized BMM agents plus BMad Master**, detailed in the [Agents Guide](./agents-guide.md). Key agent types include:

- **Quick Flow Solo Dev Agent** - Handles spec, development, and optional review for smaller tasks
- **TEA Agent** - Manages testing, quality assurance, and test healing
- **Party Mode Agents** - 19+ agents collaborate across multiple modules (BMM, CIS, BMB, custom)

Each agent has defined "roles, workflows, and when to use them" with customization capabilities.

## 2. Workflow Phases and Purposes

The method organizes development into **four core phases**:

| Phase | Purpose | Key Workflows |
|-------|---------|---------------|
| **1: Analysis** | Optional exploration and research | brainstorm-project, product-brief, research |
| **2: Planning** | Scale-adaptive planning (track-based) | PRD, tech-spec, GDD, narrative, UX |
| **3: Solutioning** | Architecture and validation | architecture, create-epics-and-stories, implementation-readiness |
| **4: Implementation** | Sprint-based JIT story production | sprint-planning, create-story, dev-story, code-review |

Testing runs separately as continuous quality gates.

## 3. Recommended Project Structure

Two primary tracks:

**Greenfield (New Projects)**
- Follow full BMad Method with progressive planning through all 4 phases
- Optional rigor levels depending on planning depth desired

**Brownfield (Existing Code)**
- Documentation phase comes first
- Track selection adapts to codebase complexity
- Integration with existing patterns prioritized

**Quick Flow Alternative**
- 3-step process: spec → dev → optional review
- Ideal for bug fixes and small features

## 4. Configuration Options

Customization supported at multiple levels:

- **Adaptive approach** - "no one size fits all"; customize to project needs
- **Optional phase steps** - rigorous planning, research, ideation, validation, testing levels vary
- **Agent customization system** - modify individual agent behavior
- **Track-based planning** - choose between Quick Flow, BMad Method, or Enterprise Method
- **Party mode composition** - select which agents participate in multi-agent collaboration

## 5. Agent Interaction Patterns

Agents collaborate through:

- **Sequential workflows** - agents execute phase-specific tasks in order
- **Party Mode** - "19+ agents collaborate in real-time" for strategic, creative, and complex work
- **Multi-module integration** - BMM agents work alongside CIS, BMB, and custom modules
- **One-story-at-a-time discipline** - Phase 4 enforces focused development cycles
- **V6 Architecture** - Epics created AFTER architecture to prevent agent conflicts and improve quality

# BMAD V6 Custom Content Guide

## 1. Creating Custom Agents

**Development Approach:**
The platform supports two agent types with increasing complexity:

- **Tiny Agents**: "Personal agents designed for highly specific needs that may not be suitable for sharing," typically scoped to specific data paths or repositories.

- **Simple Agents**: Self-contained files with all configuration and prompts in one location.

- **Expert Agents**: Expand on simple agents by including "a sidecar folder containing additional resources: custom prompt files, scripts, templates, and memory files."

**Key Consideration:** When agents target specific modules, documentation recommends including module metadata for proper dependency management.

## 2. Creating Custom Workflows

Workflows function as "progressively loading sequence engines capable of performing tasks ranging from simple to complex." At their foundation, they consist of "a single or series of prompts designed to achieve a specific outcome."

Distribution occurs through slash commands or manual execution, allowing workflows to operate independently of associated agents.

## 3. File Formats and Locations

**Expert Agent Structure:**
- Main agent file (single or multiple)
- Sidecar folder named `[agentname]-sidecar`
- Sidecar contents stored in user memory location upon installation
- Metadata designation: `type: expert`

**Supported Resources:** MCP tools, skills, execution libraries, and code bundles.

## 4. Integration with Core Modules

Custom add-on modules extend existing systems through "custom agents with awareness of the target module" and access to existing module workflows, including "tool-specific features such as rulesets, hooks, subprocess prompts, subagents."

# BMAD Custom Content Installation Summary

Based on the provided documentation, here's what's available:

## 1. BMAD V6 Alpha Installation
**Not covered in this guide.** The document focuses on custom content installation for an existing BMAD setup, not initial BMAD installation procedures.

## 2. Creating Custom Agents

The guide outlines a modular approach:

**Structure for custom agents:**
```
module-code/
  module.yaml
  agents/
    agent-name/agent-name.agent.md
```

Key requirements:
- Use a "short, abbreviated name" as your module folder (e.g., `cis`)
- Include `module.yaml` in the root directory—this "drives questions for the final generated config.yaml at install target"
- Place agent definitions in an `agents/` subfolder
- Each agent gets its own subfolder with markdown files

**For standalone agents** (like a railway domain expert), add `unitary: true` to module.yaml to indicate they're "potentially unrelated items that don't depend on each other."

## 3. Configuration File Formats

The guide references `module.yaml` but **doesn't specify its detailed structure**. Key properties mentioned:
- `unitary: true` — for standalone content collections
- The file "drives questions" for generating a config.yaml during installation

## 4. Project Initialization Steps

**New project:**
1. Installer prompts: "Would you like to install a local custom module?"
2. Select 'y' and provide the module folder path

**Existing project:**
1. Run installer against project location
2. Select "Modify BMAD Installation"
3. Choose to add/modify custom modules

---

**Note:** Complete configuration schemas and V6 alpha setup aren't addressed in this document.

# BMM Agents Guide - V6 Summary

## Available Agents

1. **Analyst (Mary)** - `*/bmad:bmm:agents:analyst` - Business analysis and research
2. **PM (John)** - `/bmad:bmm:agents:pm` - Product requirements and planning
3. **Architect (Winston)** - `/bmad:bmm:agents:architect` - System architecture and technical design
4. **SM (Bob)** - `/bmad:bmm:agents:sm` - Sprint planning and story preparation
5. **DEV (Amelia)** - `/bmad:bmm:agents:dev` - Story implementation and code review
6. **Quick-Flow Solo Dev (Barry)** - `/bmad:bmm:agents:quick-flow-solo-dev` - Fast solo development
7. **TEA (Murat)** - `/bmad:bmm:agents:tea` - Test architecture and quality strategy
8. **UX Designer (Sally)** - `/bmad:bmm:agents:ux-designer` - User experience and UI design
9. **Technical Writer (Paige)** - `/bmad:bmm:agents:tech-writer` - Technical documentation

## Naming Convention

Agent identifiers follow the pattern: `/bmad:bmm:agents:[agent-role]`

## Key Roles & Responsibilities

Each agent specializes in distinct project phases. For instance, Winston handles `*create-architecture` while John manages `*create-prd`. Murat focuses on test strategy through capabilities like `*framework` and `*atdd`.

## Customization

The reference document provided does not contain information about customizing individual agents or modifying their configurations.

content.content:
# BMad Builder Documentation Extract

## 1. Creating Custom Agents

BMad Builder supports three agent architectures:

**Simple Agents** are self-contained, stateless utilities. As documented, these are "single utilities and specialized tools" implemented entirely within one YAML file.

**Expert Agents** maintain persistent knowledge through sessions. They feature "external files and data storage" alongside domain-specific logic, exemplified by tools like journal keepers.

**Module Agents** orchestrate team workflows. These provide "enterprise-grade capabilities" and integrate with larger BMad systems for complex process management.

To initiate agent creation, load the BMad Builder agent and select the `[CA]` Create Agent option, which guides you through an 11-step process spanning concept development to deployment.

## 2. Creating Custom Modules

The documentation indicates module creation involves packaging complete solutions. Modules can encapsulate:

- Industry-specific vertical solutions
- Technology stack implementations
- Business process automation
- Educational frameworks

Module agents function as "team integration" components that coordinate workflows within specific contexts. The system supports module add-on agents through integration patterns documented in examples like the security-engineer reference.

## 3. File Formats and Structure

BMad uses **step-file workflow architecture**. Key characteristics include:

- "Micro-file Approach: Each step is self-contained"
- "Just-In-Time Loading: Only current step in memory"
- Sequential enforcement preventing step skipping
- State tracking documented in frontmatter
- Append-only document growth during execution

Agent files use YAML format (`.agent.yaml`), while workflows utilize step files with standardized templates and metadata frontmatter.

## 4. Integration with BMM

BMB integrates with BMM (Build Management Module) as a peer system. The documentation states BMB components work alongside "BMad Core" framework foundations and "BMM development workflows and project management." Module agents can orchestrate within BMM contexts for comprehensive project coordination.
